channel.title: Nature Communications
channel.description:
Nature Communications is an open access, multidisciplinary journal dedicated to publishing high-quality research in all areas of the biological, health, physical, chemical and Earth sciences. Papers published by the journal aim to represent important advances of significance to specialists within each field.

We are committed to providing an efficient service for both authors and readers. Our team of independent editors make rapid and fair publication decisions. Prompt dissemination of accepted papers to a wide readership and beyond is achieved through a programme of continuous online publication.
channel.dc:rights: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.
channel.prism:copyright: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.

item.title: Lossy phononic metamaterials for valley nonreciprocity
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 05 March 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-70037-7

Non-Hermitian and nonreciprocal valley dynamics are usually enabled by gain control. Here, authors demonstrate three non-Hermitian nonreciprocal effects in a honeycomb phononic metamaterial including unidirectional transport of bulk states, valley-dependent skin effects and anomalous beam routing, by controlling coupling loss.
item.dc:title: Lossy phononic metamaterials for valley nonreciprocity
item.dc:creator: Shunda Yin
Qiuyan Zhou
Yuxiang Xi
Weiyin Deng
Wei Chen
Jiuyang Lu
Manzhu Ke
Zhengyou Liu

item.title: Re-entrant unconventional superconductivity induced by rare-earth substitution in Nd1-xEuxNiO2 thin films
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 05 March 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-70254-0

The authors provide experimental evidence that Eu substitution in the spacer layer of Nd1-xEuxNiO2 thin films enhances the superconducting gap, driving the system toward a strong-coupling regime. The Eu substitution also introduces exchange coupling between Eu 4f magnetic moments and Ni 3dx²−y² electrons, leading to magnetic-field-enhanced “re-entrant” superconductivity.
item.dc:title: Re-entrant unconventional superconductivity induced by rare-earth substitution in Nd1-xEuxNiO2 thin films
item.dc:creator: Dung Vu
Hangoo Lee
Daniele Nicoletti
Wenzheng Wei
Zheting Jin
Dmitry V. Chichinadze
Michele Buzzi
Wenxin Li
Xinhao Yang
Rongting Wu
Christopher A. Mizzi
Tiema Qian
Boris Maiorov
Alexey Suslov
Yu He
Cyprian Lewandowski
Sohrab Ismail-Beigi
Frederick J. Walker
Andrea Cavalleri
Charles H. Ahn

item.title: Graphene quantum dot membranes with tailorable pores for efficient gas separation
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 05 March 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-69938-4

By post-tuning the GQD primitive membrane through heat treatment and in-situ crosslinking of small molecule amines, the as-prepared membrane achieved excellent CO2 separation performance and was further extended to the C3H6/C3H8 separation system.
item.dc:title: Graphene quantum dot membranes with tailorable pores for efficient gas separation
item.dc:creator: Xinjing Zhang
Qiuxia Feng
Liming Zhang
Zhongwei Cao
Hongbo Li
Xuefeng Zhu
Weishen Yang

item.title: Atomic imaging for hydrogen and boron aggregates in boron-doped diamond by spectro-photoelectron holography
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 05 March 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-70231-7

Using spectro-photoelectron holography, researchers visualized hydrogen trapping sites in boron-doped diamond. The results reveal the atomic-scale origin of acceptor passivation mechanisms in semiconductors.
item.dc:title: Atomic imaging for hydrogen and boron aggregates in boron-doped diamond by spectro-photoelectron holography
item.dc:creator: Hiroto Tomita
Wataru Hosoda
Takumi Taniguchi
Hirokazu Fujiwara
Noriyuki Kataoka
Taisuke Kageura
Yoshihiko Takano
Hiroshi Kawarada
Tamio Oguchi
Takayoshi Yokoya
Tomohiro Matsushita
